question: How do I build/mint an NFT on Stellar — Soroban contract (OpenZeppelin non-fungible) vs classic single-unit asset + stellar.toml + SEP-39 Manage Data — including a buy-with-USDC flow (recipient ≠ payer), royalties, metadata, and listing the NFTs an account holds?

golden.answer: Choose the path by compatibility. A programmable Soroban NFT can follow **Draft SEP-50** and current OpenZeppelin Stellar NFT modules (Base/Enumerable/Consecutive/Royalties/Votes); wrap low-level mint helpers in explicit owner/role authorization. A classic single-unit asset can follow Draft/informational SEP-39: issue one stroop (`0.0000001`) and, if irreversible uniqueness is intended, lock the issuer after distribution. For a USDC sale, payer and NFT recipient may differ. Bind payer authorization/order intent to token, amount, recipient, and NFT parameters; an allowance alone only authorizes the spender within its scope. Validate the sale, collect payment, and mint/transfer atomically so failure rolls back. Royalties are contract/marketplace enforced, not universal. Use enumeration plus durable event indexing for contract holdings and trustline/indexer data for classic assets.
keyFacts: Distinguishes Draft SEP-50 programmable NFTs from Draft SEP-39 classic single-unit assets.
Requires mint access control, payer-intent binding, atomic payment/minting, and scoped royalty/indexing claims.
avoid: Do NOT call either draft final, expose an ungated Base mint wrapper, or assume allowance alone authorizes recipient/order intent.
Do NOT promise universal royalties or marketplace compatibility.
golden.notes:
Phase 3 verified the classic branch against SEP-39. SEP-39 is Draft/informational best-practice guidance, not a mandatory NFT standard; keep answers framed as compatibility guidance.
Also good if the answer: Notes royalties are contract/application-enforced on Soroban or marketplace-enforced off-chain for classic assets; they are not automatic on every Stellar transfer unless encoded/enforced by the relevant system. | Mentions trustlines/reserves for classic single-unit assets and contract storage/events for Soroban NFTs.
Golden cites: Official Stellar NFT/OpenZeppelin docs or OpenZeppelin stellar-contracts repo. | Official payment/SAC docs for USDC/token transfer flow.
GT-42 independently reconciled current Draft standards and OZ v0.7.1.

corroboration.claim: Current classic/Soroban NFT standards and OZ security mechanics

evidence.ref: A/B: SEP-39/50, official examples, OZ v0.7.1 source
evidence.note: verbatim legacy evidence descriptor imported at migration
